teaching_arc.entry_point: Students finished power and sum/difference rules (Topics 10–11); many still guess (fg)′ = f′g′ on products.
teaching_arc.progression: Why products differ → prerequisite pause → counterexample + misconception → theorem → x² sin x worked → x³ cos x pause → polynomial/radical/application examples → visual lab → practice ladder → optional proof → recap teasing chain rule.
teaching_arc.destination: Students state (fg)′ = fg′ + f′g, apply it confidently, and distinguish products from compositions (Topic 13).
teaching_arc.tone: Warm, direct, YouTube-friendly, build on Topics 10–11 without re-introducing basics.
methods: Pair flex_plot three-curve demos with math_solution_steps on the left.
Name f′g′ trap before students memorize the wrong shortcut.
Move probe x when narrating factor contributions.

audience_level: first-year university / advanced high school, completed Topics 10–11
topic_goal: Teach the product rule as fg′ + f′g with worked examples, misconception guardrails, and a visual lab, teasing the chain rule for compositions.
topic_takeaways: (fg)′ = f g′ + f′ g, a sum, never f′ g′.
Label f and g before differentiating; both terms usually survive.
Use constant-multiple or expand-first shortcuts when they apply.
Compositions f(g(x)) need the chain rule (Topic 13), not the product rule.
style_notes: Welcome back after Topics 10–11, do NOT re-introduce Arian from scratch.
Roadmap: Topics 10–11 completed, Topic 12 current, Topic 13 chain rule next.
Callback power rule and sum rule on s02 prerequisite pause.
s03 misconception_compare + compact math_solution_steps for x·x² counterexample.
s04 theorem_box is the formal statement, read once, then memory aid.
s05–s10: math_solution_steps left, unique flex_plot right per slide.
s11 visual_lab: demo tab A on video; site tries all three product pairs.
pause_and_reveal on s02, s06, s12, s14, real think beats.
Avatar low on dense math_solution_steps and visual_lab slides.
transition_style: One beat per slide: name the trap, state the theorem, walk the steps, pause for practice, recap with chain-rule preview.

slides.narration_seed: Welcome back, Topics 10–11 handled sums and powers; Topic 12 is genuine products.
slides.transition_from_previous: Topic 11 ended with sum/difference; multiplication is the new frontier.
slides.delivery_goal: Students know why Topic 12 exists after Topics 10–11.

slides.narration_seed: Roadmap current Topic 12. Four skills: state formula, apply, decide when, avoid f′g′ trap.
slides.transition_to_next: Why product rule hook with three curves.

slides.narration_seed: Sum rules split addends; products don't. Widget: f=x², g=sin x, green fg, not f′g′.
slides.transition_to_next: Prerequisite pause on power + sum rules.
slides.delivery_goal: Students see why a product rule is needed.

slides.narration_seed: Pause: differentiate 3x²+5. Reveal 6x. Widget pairs f and f′.

slide_ref.source_title: Why Can't We Just Multiply Derivatives?

slides.narration_seed: misconception_compare: x·x² gives h′=3x² but f′g′=2x. Widget marks gap at x=1.
slides.delivery_goal: Students reject (fg)′ = f′g′ with a concrete counterexample.

slides.narration_seed: theorem_box: (fg)′ = fg′ + f′g. Memory aid. Widget previews fg′ and f′g term heights.
slides.delivery_goal: Students can state the product rule verbatim.

slides.narration_seed: math_solution_steps: step_1 label f,g → step_2 derivatives → step_3 apply → step_4 answer. Reveal in order.
slides.delivery_goal: Students can replicate the f/g labeling workflow.

slide_ref.source_title: Pause and Try: x³ cos x (Pause)

slides.narration_seed: Real pause. Students try x³ cos x. Reveal −x³ sin x + 3x² cos x.
slides.delivery_goal: Students attempt a similar product independently.

slides.narration_seed: splitAfter math_solution_steps for (3x²+2x)(x³−5). Verify expand-first on step_5.
slides.delivery_goal: Students handle polynomial products with expansion check.

slides.narration_seed: Rewrite √t as t^{1/2}. Product rule → factor e^t(√t + 1/(2√t)).
5x³ via product rule, f′g term vanishes. Prefer constant-multiple shortcut.
slides.delivery_goal: Students know when product rule is overkill.

slides.narration_seed: N(t)=(t²+1)e^{0.1t}. Move probe to t=3; N′(3)≈9.45.
slides.delivery_goal: Students connect product rule to rates in context.

slides.narration_seed: visual_lab three tabs: x² sin x, x e^x, (x+1) cos x. Demo tab A; site explores all.
slides.delivery_goal: Students connect formula terms to plot contributions.

slides.narration_seed: Four problems easy→challenge. pause_and_reveal hints after student attempt.
slides.delivery_goal: Students self-test before leaving the topic.

slides.narration_seed: Optional add-and-subtract proof sketch. Area strip intuition. Skippable.
slides.delivery_goal: Theory-minded students see why it's a sum.

slides.narration_seed: Recap (fg)′=fg′+f′g. Final pause: (x²)′(sin x)′ is NOT the answer, chain rule next for sin(x²).
slides.delivery_goal: Students leave with formula memory and chain-rule preview.
